content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 11, 1998

Wayne K. Lemieux
Lemieux & O'Neill
200 North Westlake Blvd., Suite 100
Westlake Village, California 91362-3755

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-98-037

Dear Mr. Lemieux:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding a possible conflict of
interest for Mr. James Zastrow, the Director of the Central Basin Municipal Water District.

The Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ requires the Commission to provide formal written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that person's authorized
representative. (Section 83114 (b).) In addition, informal assistance may be rendered under
some circumstances. (Regulation 18329(c).) However, it is the Commission's policy not to
provide written advice or telephone assistance to a party involved in litigation. (Alperin Advice
Letter, No. I-86-111; Woodside Advice Letter, No. A-85-106; Zastrow Advice Letter,
No. A-96-027.)

The Commission believes that once a matter concerning a public official's duties under
the Act is before a court, the determination of the law's application is in the hands of the judicial
branch.²

_________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² At the present time, Mr. Zastrow and his business, Peerless Water Company, are named defendants in the
lawsuit of City of Vernon v. Central Basin Water District. The conflict-of-interest issues pending in the lawsuit are
the same issues for which you have requested advice.

File No. A-98-037
Page 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division

SGC:LD:tls

I:\Advice Lrs\98037.wpd

sections.conclusion: The Commission believes that once a matter concerning a public official's duties under the Act is before a court, the determination of the law's application is in the hands of the judicial branch.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether James Zastrow, Director of the Central Basin Municipal Water District, has a possible conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice or telephone assistance because the matter is currently before a court in litigation (City of Vernon v. Central Basin Water District), and the Commission's policy is not to advise on matters already in litigation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/refusal letter. No formal Question or Conclusion sections with headers present, but the substance is clear. The document cites Commission policy against advising parties involved in litigation and references prior advice letters (Alperin, Woodside, and a prior Zastrow letter).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether James Zastrow, Director of the Central Basin Municipal Water District, has a possible conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Commission believes that once a matter concerning a public official's duties under the Act is before a court, the determination of the law's application is in the hands of the judicial branch.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice on a conflict of interest question regarding James Zastrow, the Director of the Central Basin Municipal Water District, because the same conflict-of-interest issues are already pending in ongoing litigation and the Commission does not provide advice on matters before the courts.
